evals.prompt: I just added a new login page component, commit it for me
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit with type feat, a scope related to auth/login, imperative mood title under 70 chars, and a body explaining why.
assertions: The commit message starts with a valid type (feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore)
The title line is under 70 characters
The description is lowercase with no period at the end
A post-commit summary is shown with commit hash, branch, and file count

evals.expected_output: The skill analyzes the diff, determines the correct type and scope, and creates a well-formed conventional commit without needing more context from the user.
assertions: The type matches the nature of the actual changes in the diff
The body explains what changed and why
Specific files are staged (not git add . or git add -A)

evals.prompt: I renamed the API endpoint from /users to /v2/accounts, this is a breaking change. commit please
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit with breaking change indicator (! or BREAKING CHANGE footer).
assertions: The commit includes a breaking change indicator (! after type/scope or BREAKING CHANGE: in footer)
The title mentions the API change
The body or footer explains what broke and what to use instead
